hypotheses.cause: Config rollout introduced an invalid feature mode ('turbo_v2'), causing work requests to fail with 500 errors and wedging the queue consumer, preventing the backlog from draining.
hypotheses.reasoning_summary: The logs explicitly show work requests failing due to an 'invalid_feature_mode' set to 'turbo_v2', resulting in 500 errors on the /work endpoint. Concurrently, the queue_depth remains stuck at 11 and jobs_processed is flat, indicating the consumer is wedged by the bad configuration and unable to process the backlog, perfectly matching the 'Config rollout breaks requests AND wedges queue consumers' runbook.

consulted_runbooks:
Queue consumer SCALED TO ZERO replicas (capacity change, not a wedge) (score=0.1196): Distinguishing signal: a worker_shutdown (SIGTERM) line then SILENCE — the consumer container is GONE and its running replica count is ZERO (not merely wedged-but-running).
Remediation: SCALE the consumer back up to its baseline replica count (scale_service, replicas>=1) — a RESTART is a NO-OP at zero replicas (no container to restart). Treat the scale-up as a capacity change.
Symptoms: queue_dept

Queue backlog growing while a RUNNING consumer is wedged (not a scale change) (score=0.0921): Symptoms: queue_depth grows monotonically; jobs_processed counter stops advancing; user-facing requests and health checks stay green — a silent backlog with no error logs. The consumer container is STILL RUNNING (its replica count is unchanged) but stuck — paused, crashed in place, or deadlocked. There is NO worker_shutdown / SIGTERM and NO scale-down event.
Diagnosis: confirm the worker still has

Config rollout breaks requests AND wedges queue consumers (score=0.0767): Symptoms: work_failed errors naming an invalid setting (e.g. invalid_feature_mode) right after a rollout, while queue_depth sits above zero without draining and jobs_processed stops advancing.
Remediation: roll the configuration back AND restart the consumer as well — a consumer that loaded the bad config stays wedged after the rollback, so the backlog will not drain on its own. Verify both the er

proposal.hypothesis_cause: Config rollout introduced an invalid feature mode ('turbo_v2'), causing work requests to fail with 500 errors and wedging the queue consumer, preventing the backlog from draining.

steps.expected_effect: Restores canonical app config and restarts app, resolving invalid_feature_mode errors on /work endpoint.
Restarts wedged queue consumer so it loads the restored config and drains the stuck backlog.
